observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management the following:

Assess whether there is a need to reconstitute the composition of the Inventory Committee to ensure that the task shall be completed;                                                                    

Conduct the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances in accordance with the guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006;  

Prepare of the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) using the prescribed format upon completion of the physical and furnish the Auditor concerned with the copy thereafter; and 

Conduct reconciliation between inventory count per RPCPPE and property and accounting records, as warranted.
We recommended Management to ensure that all subsequent collections made shall be deposited intact during the day or not later than the next banking day

We recommended Management to:

Formulate a policy on the establishment of cut-off in claiming the amount due to place a sense of urgency on the part of the claimant, otherwise all unrelease claims shall be returned to the local treasury and a separate voucher shall be made thereafter;

Evaluate whether there is a need to designate a Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) for offices the requires frequent cash advance such as Office of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO), Rural Health Unit (RHU) and Office of the Municipal Tourism Officer; and      

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen control over the grant of cash advance regardless of its purpose by ensuring that the requirement of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and Item 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the limitation of cash advance shall be strictly observed.

We recommended Management the following:

Municipal Treasurer to come up with an action plan to strictly observe the timeline in the submission of the RCI together with paid DVs, RCD together with accountable forms issued to the Municipal Accountant; and

Municipal Accountant to come up with an action plan to strictly observe the timeline in the submission of the RCI together with paid disbursement vouchers, RCD together with duplicate copies of accountable forms, ROD together with paid payrolls, and Liquidation Reports to the auditor concerned.

We recommended Management to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to assess the existing procedures in the preparation of contract and purchase order as well as the submission of the same to the auditor concerned and make the necessary corrective actions on the gaps noted to ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended Management the following:

Formulation of policy relative to the acceptance of delivered items taking into consideration the delegation of personnel and the specific procedures to be followed which will serve as a unified guidelines for compliance within the LGU direct the personnel in-charge of accepting the goods to notify the auditor concerned of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance thereof in compliance with pertinent provision of COA Circular No. 95-006; and

Direct the personnel in-charge of inspecting delivered goods to prepare and furnish the auditor concerned with the copy of the inspection report within the 24 hours from acceptance thereof in compliance with pertinent provision of COA Circular No. 95-006.

Recommendation

We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that Municipal Accountant:
Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect their correct treatment as liquidation of trust liabilities and correct the overstatement in the Transfers from National Government account and Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses; and
In future transactions of the same nature, revisit and apply the appropriate accounting treatment of fund transfers and their liquidation in accordance with the MOA and applicable IPSAS and COA accounting guidelines, to serve as a basis for the proper recording of similar transactions in the future.

Recommendation

We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Review the lapsing schedule and update/recompute PPE depreciation; and
Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the depreciation expense, accumulated depreciation and net book values of the affected PPE accounts.

We recommended Management to:

Conduct a thorough review of the lapsing schedule to verify accuracy and identify discrepancies in PPE depreciation.
Reconciliation and verification of depreciation schedules prior to adjustment.

Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct depreciation expense, accumulated depreciation and net book values of the affected PPE accounts.

Recommendation

We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure the following:
Constitute the Inventory Committee tasked to conduct the regular semestral physical inventory count of supplies and materials.
Municipal Accountant:
Together with the RHU supplies custodians and the Inventory Committee, to conduct physical inventory count of supplies and materials to establish the inventory balance on hand in the books of accounts; and
Recognize drugs and medicines and medical, dental and laboratory supplies expense based on the SSMI submitted by the RHU.
Rural Health Office:
Submit monthly SSMI to the Accounting Section as the basis for the recognition of supplies expenses.
Inventory Committee:
Formulate guidelines and procedures on how to conduct the semestral physical inventory to include all inventories held for consumption and issuance to end-users;
Set the timelines for the physical count, preparation and submission of the required reports to facilitate reconciliation; and
Conduct complete physical count on all inventories semi-annually and to submit the RPCI to Accounting Section as required by NGAS.

Recommendation

We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that Municipal Accountant:
Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to close the Biological Assets account amounting to ₱0.900 million and correct the overstatement in the Government Equity account by the same amount; and
In future transactions of the same nature, revisit and apply the appropriate accounting treatment of fund transfers and their liquidation in accordance with the MOA and applicable IPSAS and COA accounting guidelines, to serve as a basis for the proper recording of similar transactions in the future.

We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that the:
Municipal Accountant record expenses according to the nature of the transactions involved to prevent accounts misstatement and ensure the fair presentation of the financial statements; and
Finance Review Committee utilize historical data and exercise due diligence in identifying the appropriate accounts in accordance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-009 and IPSAS 1 during the review process and discuss with preparers of budget proposals the correct account classification to anticipate and mitigate the occurrence of unbudgeted expenditures

Recommendation

We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure the completion of the one-time cleansing of PPE by undertaking the following:
Inventory Committee to:
Coordinate with the Accounting Office to update the physical inventory to include all PPE items as of December 31, 2024, pursuant to Item 6.2 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006.
Property Section and Municipal Accounting Office to
Complete the reconciliation of the physical inventory results with the accounting records, and undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records, in accordance with Section 6.3 of the Circular; and
Undertake the necessary disposition procedures for all identified non-existent/missing PPEs, in accordance with Section 7 of the Circular.
If the non-existing/missing PPEs are established to have no available records of accountability, initiate the process of requesting authority from COA to derecognize non-existent/missing PPEs, pursuant with the guidelines prescribed under Section 8 of the Circular.

Recommendation

We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure the following:
All subsequent collections made shall be deposited on the same day or not later than the next banking day; and
Increase the bond of the Municipal Treasurer, as necessary, to ensure that government funds are covered in the event of loss, in compliance with Section 7.5 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

Recommendation

We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that the:
Authorized Agency Officer promptly and regularly update employee records in the GSIS Web MSP, especially salary adjustments, in accordance with Section B.1.d of GSIS MC No. 027-2021.
Municipal Accountant to:
Coordinate with the GSIS to reconcile the LGU's records with those of the GSIS, especially for withheld premiums and prior years’ balances; and
Thereafter, facilitate the prompt remittance of all unremitted balances, including prior years’ amounts, to the GSIS to ensure compliance with RA No. 8291.

Recommendation

We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Conduct an analysis to determine the most economical and practical approach to ensure improved efficiency and timely liquidation of cash advances considering the following approaches:
Increase the maximum cash accountability covered by the bond for the Municipal Treasurer and SDO, as necessary, in order to ensure that the funds are recovered in case of loss and in compliance with Section 7.5 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
Designate a Special Disbursing Officer for offices the requires frequent cash advances such as Office of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO), RHU and Office of the Municipal Tourism Officer reducing the burden and exposure of any single AO and allowing for faster liquidation of smaller, purpose-specific advances; or,
Require employees/payees to open individual ATM accounts, enabling the direct deposit of salaries and other recurring benefits, significantly reducing the need for cash advances and saving costs for premiums for fidelity bond.
After the conduct of the analysis, adopt a policy to guide process owners on the proper procedures on cash advances;
Implement the adopted policy and monitor the effectivity of the approach deemed most efficient and effective with the LGU.

Recommendation

We reiterated our recommendation, and Management agreed to ensure the following:
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to come up with an action plan and set internal deadlines for the timely submission of financial transaction documents; and
Strictly adhere to the prescribed timeline in the submission of the DVs, ORs, payrolls and liquidation reports, along with their corresponding reports - RCIs and RCDs in accordance with 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate preparation of necessary adjusting entry to correct the misstatement in the Cash in Bank account.

We recommended Management to:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the data in the taxpayer's index card and the assessment roll before the close of each year and submit the reconciled data to the Municipal Accountant as basis in the establishment of RPT and SET Receivables; and

Direct the Accountant to ensure that RPT and SET Receivables shall be established at the start of each year.
We recommended Management to ensure that all expenditure charged against the unexpended balance of LDRRMF of previous years shall be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended Management the following:

The Local Finance Committee to assess the current budget and make the necessary proposal to the Local Chief Executive and Sangguniang Bayan for the possible source of funds to finance the payment of salaries of JO and contract of service (COS) personnel assigned at LDRRM Office and immediately stop charging the same against the LDRRM; and 

The LDRRM Council to ensure that programs, projects, and activities to be included in the LDRRMFIP shall be consistent with the guidelines set forth in the above cited provisions.

We recommended management the following:

Establishment of cut-off time for the remittance of collections for field collectors which will provide ample time for the preparation of RCDs and for the deposits to be made taking into consideration the banking hours; and

Moving forward, direct the Municipal Treasurer to sustain the efforts in depositing all collections intact during the day or not later than the next banking day.

We recommended management the following:

Consider direct deposit to the respective account of the concerned officials as a method for payment for RATA to reduce the number of cash advances to be made; and

Moving forward, direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that previous cash advance regardless of its purpose shall be settled first, otherwise the processing of the disbursement voucher for the grant of additional cash shall be put on abeyance.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate preparation of necessary adjusting entry to correct the misstatement in the Cash in Bank account.

Recommendation:
Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all subsequent collections made shall be deposited intact during the day or not later than the next banking day.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Formulate a policy on the establishment of cut-off period in claiming the amount due to place a sense of urgency on the part of claimant, otherwise all unrelease claims shall be returned to the local treasury and a separate voucher shall be made thereafter;
Evaluate whether there is a need to designate a Special Disbursing Officer for offices the requires frequent cash advance such as Office of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO), Rural Health Unit (RHU) and Office of the Municipal Tourism Officer; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen control over the grant of cash advance regardless of its purpose by ensuring that the requirement of Section 89 of PD 1445 and Item 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the limitation of cash advance shall be strictly observed.

Recommendations:
Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal:
Treasurer to reconcile the data in the taxpayer’s index card and the assessment roll before the close of each year and submit the reconciled data to the Municipal Accountant as basis in the establishment of RPT and SET Receivables; and
Accountant to ensure that RPT and SET Receivables shall be established at the start of each year.

Recommendation:
Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all expenditures charged against the unexpended balance of LDRRMF of previous years shall be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.

Recommendations:
Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Local Finance Committee to assess the current budget and make the necessary proposal to the Local Chief Executive and Sangguniang Bayan for the possible source of funds to finance the payment of salaries of JO and COS personnel assigned at LDRRM Office and immediately stop charging the same against the LDRRMF; and
The LDRRM Council to ensure that programs, projects, and activities to be included in the LDRRMFIP shall be consistent with the guidelines set forth in the above cited provisions.

Recommendations:
Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Municipal Treasurer to come up with an action plan to strictly observe the timeline in the submission of the RCI together with paid disbursement vouchers, RCD together with accountable forms issued to the Municipal Accountant; and
Municipal Accountant to come up with an action plan to strictly observe the timeline in the submission of the RCI together with paid disbursement vouchers, RCD together with duplicate copies of accountable forms, ROD together with paid payrolls, and Liquidation Reports to the auditor concerned.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to assess the existing procedures in the preparation of contract and purchase order as well as the submission of the same to the auditor concerned and make the necessary corrective actions on the gaps noted to ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

Recommendations:
Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Formulation of policy relative to the acceptance of delivered items taking into consideration the delegation of personnel and the specific procedures to be followed which will serve as a unified guidelines for compliance within the LGUDirect the personnel in-charge of accepting the goods to notify the auditor concerned of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within twenty-four hours from acceptance thereof in compliance with pertinent provision of COA Circular No. 95-006 and COA Circular No. 96-010; and
Lastly, direct the personnel in-charge of inspecting delivered goods to prepare and furnish the auditor concerned with the copy of the inspection report within twenty-four hours from acceptance thereof in compliance with pertinent provision of COA Circular No. 95-006.

We recommended management the following:


Direct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the property/supply officer to determine whether the related goods and services in relation to the recorded accounts payable has been delivered/rendered, otherwise cause the derecognition of the same; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to drop from the accounting records, the liability relative to the unclaimed financial assistance amounting to ₱21,000.00.
We recommend management to conduct interim assessment to determine the extent of implementation of GAD PPAs and propose corrective measure to address the gaps and/or lapses noted.

We recommended management the following:

The GFPS – TWG to consider other source of sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information as provided under Item 4.1 (B)2 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and

Strengthening of the GAD Focal Point System by capacitating the members of the GFPS Executive Committee and GFPS Technical Working Group through capacity building programs on GAD which shall include but not be limited to attendance to various training/seminars such as gender sensitivity training (GST), gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, and GAD tools such as the Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework (GMEF), Gender Responsive LGU Ka Ba (GeRL Ka Ba) Self-Assessment Tool, Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) and other GAD tools through attendance to seminar, training and other related capacity development activities.

Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to coordinate in identifying these PPEs and to depreciate them accordingly.

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to record and adjust the unrecorded prior years’ depreciation expenses. 

Moving forward to the issuance of COA Circular 2020-006, which prescribes the guidelines and procedures on inventory taking, recognition of those found at station and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to implement the guidelines and procedures in order to clean up these PPE accounts. 

Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, to strictly follow the provisions for Depreciation of PPE as described in sections 260 – 262.

The Municipal Accountant reclassify all completed projects in the CIP account to the appropriate asset account and record the accumulated depreciation from prior years and depreciate these assets accordingly; and 

Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs Volume I, follow the illustrated entries in section 251 item (g) to record the construction of PPEs.

The LGU comply with the pertinent requirements and regulations concerning the DILG-LGSF-AM Projects; and 

Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit all the required reports.

The Local Chief Executive to direct the completion of the annual physical count of PPEs and Inventories and come up with the RPCPPE and RPCI and accordingly prepare the PIF to be submitted to the Audit Team and GSIS to secure insurance.

The Municipal Treasurer be directed to prepare the Local Road Network Property Card, Local Road Network Ledger Card, Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and the Report on Local Road Network; 

The Municipal Accountant to maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical count at year end and to properly depreciate the account; and to properly disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

The Municipal Assessor to communicate with the Office of the Provincial Assessor to initiate the province wide assessment and to plan and implement the expected update of the Assessment Rolls as to the conditions of the real properties, its actual use, and the exemptions on RPT, if any, and to regularly conduct general revision of assessment of real property every three (3) years in accordance with the existing laws, rules and regulations.

Prepare a 10-year solid waste management plan that is in accordance with the national solid waste management framework for the reuse, recycling and composting of wastes generated in the municipality. It should contain all the components provided by this Act to ensure the approval of the National Solid Waste Management Commission, thus ensuring the efficient management of solid and residual wastes generated within its jurisdiction. Further, the plan should also be reviewed and updated every year by the municipal solid waste management board.

The Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to regularly update and maintain Property Cards, issue the necessary PAR for those issued PPEs under the accountability of the end users and to renew the same every three (3) years or as soon as there is a change in custody as required in the PSM Manual to strengthen control and accountability over government assets; and 

Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs to strictly follow the provisions for Accounting and Property Records to be maintained for PPE as described in Section 269 of Volume I.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System:

Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered age- and sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; 

Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and
Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for gender issues.
Establishment of GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
We recommended that the LGU maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided.

Management to immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the Buildings owned and so that no fines, penalties, abatements or closures are imposed on the agency.

The LSB Chairman to:

Follow the timeline in preparing the SEF budget;

Meet at least monthly with the members of the LSB to address the issues in the education system in the public sector, and in coordinated efforts plan the allocation of the SEF largely on the priority projects enumerated under JC No. 01; and

Include allocations from their SEF for the ECCD PPAs.

The Local Chief Executive require the Local Economic Enterprise Officer to prepare the 5-Year Staffing Plan and Staff Transition Strategy and Five (5) Year Business Plan for each Local Economic Enterprise; and 

Also, prepare the semi-annual SAFSSR needed to support the FFSE.
Management to strictly adhere with the requirements and provisions of existing laws concerning LCPC and to submit the required reports.

The Local Finance Committee to identify additional sources of income and/or push for the passage of updated revenue code to boost local taxation and design a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue collection based on the current state of the Municipality’s economic enterprises; and 

The Municipal Treasurer to monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts from Service and Business sources.
The Local Chief Executive improve the internal control system of the LGU by addressing the gaps presented.

Management to provide basis for the transfer of water works operation to SBRI; and
Submission of the following documents to enable us to make a decision in audit:

LGU P4 Code
Comprehensive Development Plan

The Municipal Accountant to analyze and reconcile the contribution premiums withheld for the GSIS and to remit immediately what was due to GSIS in accordance with the existing laws, rules, and regulations; and

Also, for the municipality to remit directly to the GSIS the members/employees contributions within the first ten (10) days of the calendar month following the month to which the contributions apply.
The Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the unremitted withheld taxes to the BIR, representing the withheld VAT and income taxes from government purchases.

Recommendations
We recommended management the following:
Direct the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere with the requirement of forwarding the Report of Check Issued (RCI) including information about unreleased check/s and cancelled check/s during the day, if any on a daily basis to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; and
Direct the office of the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of deviating the recording process but instead adhere with the requirement that the RCI shall be the basis for the preparation of JEV.

Recommendations
We recommended management the following:
restatement of affected financial statements upon recognition of related expenses for fuel, oil and lubricant; and
moving forward, direct the respective head of office concerned to exercise timely submission of consumption reports by setting-up deadlines.

Recommendation
We recommended management to come up with an action plan in accordance with the guidelines and specific procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 availing the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

Recommendations
We recommended management the following:
direct the Municipal Agriculturist to furnish the Office of the Municipal Accountant with document/s evidencing the distribution/dispersal of livestock to various beneficiaries; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary entry to derecognized the Biological Assets for the books of accounts based on the documents presented by the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist.

Recommendations
We recommended management to direct the municipal accountant:
to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correct the erroneous recording of accounts payable under the general fund books and submit the same to the auditor concerned;
to conduct a thorough analysis on the existence of the accounts payable under the special education fund books and trust fund books to determine the appropriateness of its recognition; and
to cause the derecognition of accounts payable as a result of the thorough analysis conducted, if circumstances warrant.

Recommendations
We recommended management to:
direct the municipal accountant to conduct a thorough analysis on the existence of abnormal balances to various accounts as identified above; and
direct the municipal accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries depending on the result of the analysis conducted, as warranted.

Recommendations
We recommended management to:
avail the one-time cleansing of PPE provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with reliable balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability; and
thereafter, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to set up depreciation on PPEs which were not depreciated in the previous years.

Recommendations
We recommended management the following:
make representation with the Office of the Provincial Assessor to undertake general revision of assessments and property classification which has been long overdue; and
make representation with the Office of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan through the Secretary to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan to pass a resolution relative to the general revision of assessments and property classification.

Recommendation
We recommended management to update the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU that is consistent with the national solid waste management framework.

Recommendations
We recommended management
to strengthen the GAD Focal Point System by capacitating the GAD Focal Person;
thereafter, direct the GAD Focal Person to establish a comprehensive GAD database that will serve as basis in planning, programming and policy formulation for gender responsive programs and activities

Recommendation
We recommended management to create GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team through issuance of Executive Order or Administrative Order in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 to assess the effectiveness and efficiency of GAD mainstreaming efforts.

Recommendation
We recommended management to direct the Local Finance Committee to come up with an action plan identifying possible additional sources of income that will augment local revenues to uplift IRA dependency of the LGU.

Recommendation
We recommended management to improve the internal control system by addressing the gaps presented in the previous sections.

Recommendations
We recommended management to
provide basis for the transfer of water works operation to South Balibago Resources Incorporated; and
submit LGU P4 Code.

The Municipal Treasurer to expediate the documents needed to support the receipt of the donated property. 

The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entry to recognize the transfer of assets from the PLGU Sibugay in the books of accounts of the LGU. 

Further, depreciate retrospectively accordingly the PPE from the moment of receipt of the assets and not during recording of the assets.
The Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary journal entry voucher (JEV) to correct the erroneous entries made.

The Municipal Accountant to reclassify the PPE as Investment Properties in accordance to IPSAS; and

Moving forward to the implementation of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs refer to Sections 287 to 303 of Volume I for the proper recording and reporting of Investment Properties.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the IPSAS 42 and to properly disclose the needed disclosures in the Financial Statements.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the IPSAS 14 and to properly disclose the needed disclosures in the Financial Statements.

The LGU comply with the pertinent requirements and regulations concerning the ESP and SAP. Also, the Local Chief Executive advise the MSWDO and Municipal treasurer to timely distribute future ESPs and liquidate the same following the prescribed timeline.

Direct the officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the prompt submission of the financial reports with complete documentation as basis in establishing the validity and regularity of the payments made. Failure on the part of the officers and employees concerned to submit the required documents and financial reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission as provided in Section 122 of PD No. 1445.

Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of year the list of all the on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year as well as the monthly monitoring report on the implementation of the PPAs. The prescribed form provided in the circular shall be used indicating in the remarks column the mode of procurement and specific status of the project such as on-going, for implementation, completed, suspended/ cancelled, terminated and for bidding.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other in order to prevent future delays in computations and preparations of pertinent accounting documents to facilitate the prompt release and to release immediately any unreleased shares to the barangays concerned.
Prioritize the processing of the titling of the lands to prove claim of ownership and to safeguard government interest.

Submission of GAD Plan and Budget and Utilization/
Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within the reglementary period provided by COA Circular 2014-001.

The Human Resource Management Officer to revise the leave balances of the affected Municipal Employees. Inform the Municipal Employees of the error for future reference since the Memorandum Order is still in effect.
We recommended that the bonds of the accountable officer be renewed before the expiration date of such.

The Municipal Treasurer to instruct the various revenue collectors to revisit the Local Government Code, the Local Treasury Manual and the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code in order to be knowledgeable of the various laws, rules and regulations concerning revenue collection; and

Further, include in the next information drive of the Municipality the need of large cattle owners to register their large cattle with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and require the registration of large cattle upon reaching the age of two (2) years, as well as the registration of sale of transfer of ownership of large cattle.

The LGU to comply with the pertinent requirements and regulations concerning the transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the SEF as required by DepEd, DBM, and DILG JC No. 01; and 

The Municipal Accountant to timely prepare and submit all the required reports.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to provide depreciation to the above PPEs and to submit the lapsing schedule of depreciation.
We recommended that the management conduct a complete actual inventory count of all its PPEs annually and reconcile the results of the count with accounting records.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be directed to prepare the Local Road Network Property Card, Local Road Network Ledger Card, Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and the Report on Local Road Network. 

The Municipal Accountant to maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical count at year end and to properly depreciate the account.
We recommended that the municipality to prioritize the processing of the titling of the lands to prove claim of ownership and to safeguard government interest.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to reclassify all completed projects in the CIP account to the appropriate asset account.

We recommended that the municipality demand for the collection or liquidation of the above receivables. The municipality may consider the rules and procedures in writing off the receivable accounts as provided in COA Circular 2016-005.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to reconcile the difference between the actual amounts presented in the SCBAA and the amount in the Statement of Financial Performance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. 

Also, the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the prescribed disclosure.

We recommended that the LGU to comply with the pertinent requirements and regulations concerning the DILG-LGSF-AM Projects. 

Also, the Local Chief Executive advise the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit all the required reports.

We recommended that the municipality prepare a 10-year solid waste management plan that is in accordance with the national solid waste management framework for the reuse, recycling and composting of wastes generated in the municipality. It should contain all the components provided by this Act to ensure the approval of the National Solid Waste Management Commission, thus ensuring the efficient management of solid and residual wastes generated within its jurisdiction. Further, the plan should also be reviewed and updated every year by the municipal solid waste management board.

We recommended that the municipal assessor update the Assessment Rolls as to the conditions of the real properties, its actual use, and the exemptions on RPT, if any, and to regularly conduct real property assessment every three (3) years in accordance with the existing laws, rules and regulations.
We recommended that the LGU establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
We recommended that the LGU maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided.
We recommended Management to strictly adhere with the requirements and provisions of existing laws concerning LCPC and to submit the required reports.
We recommended that management insure its insurable properties with the GSIS. The Municipal treasurer to prepare the lists of insurable properties to facilitate the processing of insurance with GSIS.

We recommended that management immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the Buildings owned and so that no fines, penalties, abatements or closures are imposed on the agency.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive improve the internal control system of the LGU by addressing the gaps presented.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be directed to regularly renew the PARs of the PPEs every three (3) years as required in the PSM Manual to strengthen control and accountability over government assets.

We recommended that the officials and employees concerned be directed to strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the prompt submission of the financial reports with complete documentation as basis in establishing the validity and regularity of the payments made.
